name: Aqua Security API
what_it_does: Cloud-native security platform with REST API for container image scanning, Kubernetes runtime security, vulnerability management, and compliance enforcement across the CI/CD pipeline.
use_cases: Triggering container image vulnerability scans in CI/CD pipelines via API
Querying vulnerability findings and risk scores for security agent decision-making
Enforcing image assurance policies that block non-compliant images from deployment
Monitoring runtime security events and responding to active container threats
Auditing Kubernetes workload compliance against CIS benchmarks and regulatory standards
not_for: Organizations without Kubernetes or Docker infrastructure
best_when: A security agent needs to enforce and audit container security policies across large Kubernetes deployments with strict compliance requirements.
avoid_when: You're starting with container security and need a simpler, lower-cost entry point (Trivy open-source or Snyk Container may suffice).

auth.notes: Username/password or API key generated from Aqua console. Role-based access controls limit what each API key can read or modify. Service accounts recommended for automated access.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier; 30-day trial by request
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise licensing; typically $30k-300k+/year depending on workload size
pricing.notes: Aqua competes with Palo Alto Prisma Cloud and Sysdig in enterprise container security. Pricing by workload count (nodes/containers).

performance.rate_limits: Varies by plan; documented in customer portal

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: REST API is well-documented for enterprise customers. Error responses are JSON with error codes and messages. Security policy enforcement errors include actionable remediation guidance.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Image scanning is idempotent for the same image digest. Policy creation supports idempotent updates with name-based identification.
known_agent_gotchas: Scan results are async — agent must poll for completion or use webhook notifications
Image scanning time varies by image size and vulnerability database updates (1-10 minutes)
Policy enforcement requires understanding Aqua's assurance policy model before configuration
API documentation requires customer access — not publicly available

security_score_components.security_notes: FedRAMP authorized. RBAC with service accounts for least-privilege API access. SOC2 Type II and HIPAA compliant. API communication encrypted. Enterprise security practices.
